cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What are the four essential elements of a Negligence claim?
cards.back: Duty, Breach, Actual causation, Proximate causation, and Damages.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What is the standard 'Duty of reasonable care' applied in negligence cases?
cards.back: A person must act as a reasonably prudent person would act under similar circumstances.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: A professional must exercise the skill and care of an ordinary professional in that same field.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: A child must act as a reasonable child of similar age, intelligence, and experience, unless performing an adult activity.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: Under what standard of care must common carriers and innkeepers operate toward passengers and guests?
cards.back: They are held to a heightened standard of care.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: Which legal test is usually applied to determine 'Actual causation'?
cards.back: The 'but-for' test: but for the defendant's conduct, would the injury have occurred?

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: In negligence, what is the 'Substantial factor' test used to determine?
cards.back: Actual causation in cases involving multiple sufficient causes.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: Under what conditions does 'Alternative liability' shift the burden of proof to the defendants?
cards.back: When multiple negligent defendants exist, only one caused the harm, and the plaintiff cannot determine which one.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: How is 'Proximate causation' defined in tort law?
cards.back: The harm must be a foreseeable result of the defendant's conduct.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What is the legal effect of an 'Unforeseeable superseding act' on a defendant's liability?
cards.back: It may cut off the defendant's liability for the resulting harm.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Defendant violated a statute; Plaintiff is in the protected class; Harm is the type intended to be prevented; Violation caused the injury.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Accident usually doesn't occur without negligence; Instrumentality was in defendant's control; Plaintiff did not cause the accident.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What must a doctor disclose to a patient to satisfy the requirement of 'Informed consent'?
cards.back: Material risks, alternatives, and consequences that a reasonable patient would consider important.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Special relationship; Defendant created the danger; Voluntary undertaking; Contract; Statute.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What is the duty owed to an 'Undiscovered trespasser'?
cards.back: Usually no duty except to avoid willful or wanton injury.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What is the duty owed to a 'Licensee' (social guest)?
cards.back: A duty to warn of concealed dangerous conditions known to the owner.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What is the duty owed to an 'Invitee' (business visitor)?
cards.back: A duty to reasonably inspect, discover, and correct dangerous conditions.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Dangerous artificial condition; Owner knows children trespass; Child cannot appreciate danger; Risk outweighs burden of fixing; Owner fails to use care.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Intent; Harmful or offensive contact; Contact with plaintiff's person; Causation.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Intent; Plaintiff's reasonable apprehension of imminent harmful or offensive contact; Causation.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Intent; Confinement within bounded area; Plaintiff knows of confinement or is harmed; No reasonable means of escape.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: Intentional infliction of emotional distress (IIED) elements
cards.back: Extreme and outrageous conduct; Intent or recklessness; Causation; Severe emotional distress.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Conversion involves interference so serious that the defendant must pay the property's full value.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: Under the 'Transferred intent' doctrine, for which torts can intent transfer?
cards.back: Battery, Assault, False imprisonment, Trespass to land, and Trespass to chattels.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What are the requirements for 'Self-defense' in tort law?
cards.back: A reasonable belief of imminent unlawful force and a proportional response.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Employee committed a tort within the scope of employment, intended at least partly to serve the employer.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Commercial supplier; Defective product; Defect existed when leaving defendant; No substantial change; Foreseeable use; Causation; Damages.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: The product differs from its intended design or specifications.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Defamatory statement; About plaintiff; Publication to a third person; Fault; Damages (unless presumed).

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What is the 'Actual malice' standard for defamation of public figures?
cards.back: Knowledge of the statement's falsity or reckless disregard for the truth.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Substantial and unreasonable interference with another's use and enjoyment of land.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: The defendant is liable for the full extent of the plaintiff's injuries even if they are unusually severe or unforeseeable.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What are the three essential requirements for 'Contract formation'?
cards.back: Offer, Acceptance, and Consideration.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Offer by a merchant; Signed writing; Assurance the offer will stay open.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: The acceptance must match the terms of the offer exactly, or it is treated as a counteroffer.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: A promise; Reasonable and foreseeable reliance; Actual detrimental reliance; Enforcement is necessary to avoid injustice.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Marriage; Year (over one); Land; Executor; Goods ($\$500$ or more); Suretyship.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What is the effect of an 'Anticipatory repudiation'?
cards.back: The nonbreaching party may sue immediately, suspend performance, wait for the performance date, or urge retraction.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Performance is excused because it is objectively impossible due to death, destruction, or supervening illegality.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What are 'Expectation damages' intended to achieve?
cards.back: To put the plaintiff in the position they would have been in if the contract had been fully performed.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: When is 'Specific performance' usually available as a remedy?
cards.back: When there is a valid contract with definite terms and the legal remedy (money) is inadequate, such as for land or unique goods.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: A buyer may reject goods if they fail in any respect to conform to the contract.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What are the two requirements for 'Diversity jurisdiction'?
cards.back: Complete diversity of citizenship and an amount in controversy exceeding $\$75,000$.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: How is the citizenship of a corporation determined for diversity purposes?
cards.back: By its state of incorporation and the state of its principal place of business (nerve center).

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What is the 'Well-pleaded complaint' rule for federal-question jurisdiction?
cards.back: The federal question must appear on the face of the plaintiff's complaint, not in an anticipated defense.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: Which Rule 12 defenses are waived if not raised in the defendant's first response?
cards.back: Personal jurisdiction, Venue, Insufficient process, and Insufficient service of process.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: When is 'Summary judgment' granted under Rule 56?
cards.back: When there is no genuine dispute of material fact and the movant is entitled to judgment as a matter of law.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Valid final judgment on the merits; Same parties or privies; Same claim or transaction.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Same issue; Actually litigated; Actually decided; Essential to judgment; Fair opportunity for the party against whom it is used.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Injury in fact; Causation; Redressability.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What may Congress regulate under the 'Commerce Clause'?
cards.back: Channels of interstate commerce; Instrumentalities/persons/things in interstate commerce; Intrastate activities with a substantial effect on interstate commerce.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: The government must prove the law is narrowly tailored to achieve a compelling government interest.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: The government must prove the law is substantially related to an important government interest.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: The plaintiff must show the law has no rational relationship to any legitimate government interest.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: Time, Place, and Manner restriction requirements (Public Forum)
cards.back: Content neutral; Narrowly tailored to a significant interest; Leaves open ample alternative channels.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What is the standard for 'Incitement' to be unprotected speech?
cards.back: Speech intended and likely to cause imminent unlawful action.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Aiding, encouraging, or facilitating the crime with the intent to assist the principal and the required mental state.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: An agreement; Intent to agree; Intent to achieve the unlawful objective; An overt act (in many jurisdictions).

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What are the four types of 'Malice aforethought' for common law murder?
cards.back: Intent to kill; Intent to cause serious bodily injury; Reckless indifference to human life; Felony murder.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: An intentional killing is reduced if there was adequate provocation, no cooling time, and the defendant did not actually cool off.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Trespassory taking; Carrying away; Personal property of another; Intent to permanently deprive.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Robbery requires taking from the person or presence by force or threat of immediate harm.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Due to mental disease, the defendant did not know the nature of the act or did not know it was wrong.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: Fourth Amendment: Search incident to arrest (Vehicle)
cards.back: Police may search if the arrestee is unsecured and can access the vehicle, or they reasonably believe it contains evidence of the arrest offense.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Custody (environment where one doesn't feel free to leave) and Interrogation (questioning likely to elicit an incriminating response).

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: What is the 'Blockburger' test for Double Jeopardy?
cards.back: Two crimes are different if each requires proof of an element that the other does not.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Relevant evidence may be excluded if its probative value is substantially outweighed by the danger of unfair prejudice.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.front: When can a criminal defendant offer 'Character evidence'?
cards.back: A defendant may offer reputation or opinion testimony about their own pertinent good character trait.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: An out-of-court statement offered to prove the truth of the matter asserted.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Record made at/near time; By person with knowledge; Kept in regular course; Regular practice to make record; Trustworthy.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Declarant unavailable; Believed death was imminent; Statement about cause/circumstances; Homicide or civil case.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: In a criminal case, a witness-spouse may refuse to testify against their current spouse; the witness-spouse holds the privilege.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Time, Title, Interest, and Possession.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Actual, Open and notorious, Exclusive, Hostile, and Continuous for the statutory period.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Protects against all title defects arising before and during the grantor's period of ownership.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: A later bona fide purchaser (BFP) without notice wins only if they record their deed first.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Prior common ownership and a severance that creates a landlocked parcel.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: A landlord's duty in residential leases to provide premises fit for basic human living.

cards.subject: MEE Focused
cards.back: Substantial interference caused by landlord; Tenant gave notice; Landlord failed to cure; Tenant vacated within reasonable time.
